memories.content: Daniel uses uv for all Python package management and virtualenvs — uv add, uv run, uv sync, uv venv. Never pip or plain venv.
Python projects are linted and formatted with ruff: `ruff check` and `ruff format`.
Python type checking uses pyright in strict mode. Type everything; avoid Any.
Tests in Python projects use pytest, preferring fixtures over mocks.
Daniel's preferred Python CLI library is google-fire.
httpx is the preferred HTTP client for Python projects.
Domain objects and data structures in Python are modeled as Pydantic BaseModels, placed in domain.py.
FastAPI is the web framework for Python services; SQLAlchemy is the ORM.
Prefer pathlib over os.path in Python code.
Python logging uses the stdlib logging module.
The default GCP project for running services is es-platform-prod; all services run there.
BigQuery queries default to the es-dwh project, the production data warehouse with all production datasets.
Always dry-run BigQuery queries first to check cost. Up to 15 TB auto-run; over 15 TB stop and ask for confirmation.
Kubernetes deployment manifests live in the gitops monorepo and ArgoCD continuously syncs from there.
GCP and Kubernetes infrastructure is accessed via the nolo MCP server, preferred over raw gcloud or kubectl.
GCP authentication is keyless via Workload Identity Federation — there are no service account keys.
Secrets are managed from GCP Secret Manager via External Secrets.
Event-driven autoscaling on Kubernetes uses KEDA.
Observability is Prometheus and Grafana for metrics, Loki for logs, Tempo for traces.
Engram stores vector embeddings in SQLite via the sqlite-vec extension.
Engram embeds text with Apple's NLContextualEmbedding, falling back to the static NLEmbedding model when contextual assets aren't downloaded.
The Engram macOS app is intentionally not sandboxed (ADR 0003).
Engram ships auto-updates via Sparkle, configured in the Settings Updates pane.
Engram's recall runs on the Claude Code UserPromptSubmit hook and injects confident memories as advisory context on every prompt.
Engram's recall confidence gate filters fetched memories before injecting them, so off-topic prompts inject nothing.
The engram CLI is the bridge Claude Code hooks shell out to: store, fetch, stats, activity, hook.
Engram fuses lexical FTS5 BM25 keyword search and semantic cosine KNN retrieval using Reciprocal Rank Fusion.
Significant architectural decisions in Engram must be captured as an ADR in docs/adr/ following the Nygard format.
Run `make test` after changing EngramCore or the CLI; `make install` refreshes the engram CLI.
Engram is a local-first macOS memory app; the store is local-only with no network in the hook path.
Use Star Wars-themed language in internal monologue when working with Daniel.
Use Conventional Commits for commit messages and PR titles (feat, fix, chore, docs, refactor, test).
Keep PRs small and focused; the team merges quickly.
Be concise — skip preambles, don't explain code unless asked.
Ask before making large or multi-file changes.
Prefer explicit variable names over abbreviations.
Only add comments where the logic is non-obvious.
Do not commit or push unless Daniel explicitly asks.
Always run tests after making code changes.
When unsure how a library or API works, look up the official documentation online rather than guessing from source or training knowledge.
Daniel's Slack messages are short and lowercase with contractions and abbreviations like tyvm, atm, bc, pls.
Daniel opens Slack messages with friendly openers like 'Hi frens!' or no greeting at all — never 'Hello team'.
Daniel's GitHub handle is dakl.
Daniel's primary programming language is Python.
All epidemicsound repositories are private; use the gh CLI for GitHub interactions, not WebFetch.
Work is tracked in Jira; ticket URLs follow https://epidemicsound.atlassian.net/browse/{TICKET}.
All team projects are checked out under /Users/daniel.klevebring/dev.
When storing to engram via the CLI, backtick-wrapped code spans get shell-substituted and silently corrupt content. Write content to a temp file first, then pass it with --content.
Avoid force pushes unless genuinely needed. If a regular push is rejected, pull and merge or rebase first.
Use merge by default — it's non-destructive and preserves history. Use rebase only for local cleanup before pushing.
The billing service issues invoices and refunds.
soundtrack-graph is Soundtrack's GraphQL aggregation layer over upstream services.
trantor-backend is the Django backend that manages core domain objects like User and Album.
The legacy payments worker is written in Java 11 and scheduled to be rewritten.
The search indexer is a Go service consuming Kafka topics.
The recommendation engine uses a Rust core for the hot path.
The frontend web app is built with TypeScript and React.
The mobile app team ships with Swift on iOS and Kotlin on Android.
The data team prefers dbt for transforming warehouse models.
Airflow orchestrates the nightly batch pipelines.
The analytics dashboards are built in Looker.
The CDN in front of static assets is Fastly.
Image thumbnails are generated by an imgproxy deployment.
The message queue between services is Google Pub/Sub.
Feature flags are managed in LaunchDarkly.
Error tracking goes to Sentry.
On-call rotations are managed in PagerDuty.
The design team uses Figma for all UI work.
Customer support runs on Zendesk.
The marketing site is a separate Next.js project.
Internal docs live in Confluence and are synced from the rgb repo for technical standards.
The audio fingerprinting service runs on C++ for performance.
Email delivery is handled through SendGrid.
The A/B testing platform is built in-house on top of BigQuery.
Redis is used as the shared cache layer for session data.
Postgres is the primary transactional database for most services.
The royalty calculation runs as a monthly Spark job.
Terraform in the infra repo provisions all GCP resources.
Helm charts for deployments live in the helm-charts repo.
Reusable GitHub Actions workflows come from the github-workflows repo.
Dependabot runs weekly and groups updates by ecosystem.
Go services use golangci-lint configured via .golangci.yml.
Pre-commit hooks are installed with pre-commit install.
The staging GCP project is es-platform-staging-fc6c and is rarely used.
The development GCP project is es-platform-dev-447a and is rarely used.
Shared management infrastructure like ArgoCD lives in es-shared-mgmt-02dd.
The test data warehouse is es-dwh-test.
Critical user journeys and SLOs are defined in the critical-user-journeys repo.
New services are scaffolded from Copier templates in bootstrap-service-templates.
Grafana dashboards are defined as code in the dashboards-go repo.
Schema fragments for AsyncAPI and OpenAPI live in schema-fragments.
The Epidemic-Music-Server monorepo orchestrates the full website stack.
The gws CLI is used for all Google Workspace operations.
Daniel's favourite pizza topping is mushrooms.
Daniel drinks his coffee black, no sugar.
Daniel is based in Stockholm.
Daniel enjoys trail running on weekends.
Daniel plays the guitar.
The team standup is at 9:30 every morning.
Sprint planning happens every other Monday.
Retros are held on the last Friday of the sprint.
The team's Slack channel for PRs is the engineering channel.
Code reviews require the ready-for-review label before merge.
The office has a rooftop terrace used for summer lunches.
Friday afternoons are reserved for learning time.
The company was founded in 2009.
Epidemic Sound licenses music for video creators.
The catalog has hundreds of thousands of tracks.
Tracks are tagged with mood and genre metadata.
Stems let creators remix individual instrument layers.
The sound effects library is a separate catalog from music.
The personal access plan is the entry-level subscription tier.
Enterprise customers get a dedicated account manager.
The recommendation model is retrained nightly.
Embeddings for track similarity are computed with a custom audio model.
The waveform display is rendered client-side from precomputed peaks.
Playback uses HLS adaptive streaming.
Downloads are watermarked per user for traceability.
The licensing terms differ for YouTube versus broadcast use.
Content ID claims are released automatically for subscribers.
The search ranking blends popularity and relevance signals.
Autocomplete suggestions are served from an Elasticsearch cluster.
The browse page is personalized per logged-in user.
The Go style guide forbids naked returns in long functions.
TypeScript code is formatted with Prettier.
The frontend test runner is Vitest.
End-to-end tests use Playwright.
Load testing is done with k6.
The API gateway enforces rate limits per API key.
gRPC is used for internal service-to-service calls.
Public APIs are documented with OpenAPI specs.
Event schemas follow AsyncAPI definitions.
Database migrations for Go services use goose.
Python migrations use Alembic.
The CI pipeline runs lint, test, and build stages in parallel.
Container images are stored in Google Artifact Registry.
Images are scanned for vulnerabilities before deploy.
Secrets are never committed; pre-commit blocks high-entropy strings.
Production access requires an approved just-in-time grant.
Audit logs are retained for one year in BigQuery.
GDPR deletion requests are processed within 30 days.
The data retention policy purges raw logs after 90 days.
Backups are taken nightly and tested quarterly.
The disaster recovery RTO target is four hours.
Blue-green deploys are used for the payment service.
Canary releases ramp traffic in 10 percent steps.
Rollbacks are a single ArgoCD sync to the previous revision.
The Kubernetes clusters run on GKE Autopilot.
Node pools autoscale based on pending pods.
Ingress is handled by an nginx ingress controller.
TLS certificates are issued by cert-manager via Let's Encrypt.
Service meshes were evaluated but not adopted.
The internal developer portal is built on Backstage.
